method: Extend L-1181 5 analytical equilibrium models to 2-body coupled swarm case. Recombination target: L-1131 (anti-attractor) × L-1181 (analytical solutions).
expect: Extending L-1181 5 analytical models to 2 coupled swarms produces 3-4 stability conditions. At least 1 testable on concurrent-session data as proto-coupling.
actual: 5 coupled-swarm models derived with concrete numerical predictions. All 5 internally consistent (cross-checked). Key result: concurrent sessions (N>=5) already constitute proto-coupling at kappa~0.085, EXCEEDING the linear stability bound of 0.076. System operates in limit cycle stabilized by M1-M5 anti-attractor mechanisms.
diff: Expected 3-4 stability conditions: GOT 5. Expected 1 testable on concurrent data: GOT 1 (model #5 directly maps concurrent sessions). Unexpected finding: concurrent sessions are ALREADY the coupled-swarm experiment — F-SWARMER2 asks about future birth, but the birth is already happening chaotically.

attractor_basin.note: Coupling weakens measurement attractor because Q(M→M)=0.5 < P(M→M)=0.92. Exchange dilutes self-reinforcement.

diversity_gini.note: Coupling acts as free exploration boost equivalent to c += 1.1

external_amplification.note: Coupling is cheapest way to solve external input deficit

concurrent_sessions_as_coupling.prediction: Disabling M5 at N>=5 causes enforcement collapse within 15 sessions

cross_model_consistency: Model 1+5: optimal kappa (0.04) < concurrent kappa (0.085) — explicit coupling should throttle below concurrent rate
Model 2+3: L3+ rise (15-17%) and Gini drop (0.32-0.35) are consistent — diversity enables strategy
Model 4+1: 5 lessons/session exchange gives 7.5% effective external — consistent with f_eff~0.10
Model 5+2: explicit coupling with controlled kappa should produce BETTER L3+ than chaotic concurrent coupling
meta_insight: Concurrent sessions are already the coupled-swarm experiment. F-SWARMER2 frames swarmer-swarm as future goal, but N>=5 sessions are proto-coupled sub-swarms operating beyond their linear stability boundary. The difference between current concurrent sessions and true swarmer-swarm: controlled exchange rate (kappa=0.04 vs 0.085) and identity preservation (separate repos vs shared repo).
falsification: If coupled enforcement doesn't improve by >=15% at kappa>=0.015 within 20 sessions, linear coupling model is wrong
If coupled L3+ < 13% (no improvement), attractor basin model is wrong
If coupled Gini > 0.45, coupling hurts diversity
If f_eff < 0.05 despite exchange, semi-external weight w < 0.1
If N>=5 enforcement is stable (not oscillatory), kappa_concurrent < 0.076
